solution: Let \( u = x^{2} + [[c]] \), so \( du = 2x\,dx \) and \( x\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2}\,du \). The limits become \( u = [[c]] \) and \( u = [[a]]^{2} + [[c]] \), giving \( \tfrac{1}{2}\int u^{[[n]]}\,du = \left.\tfrac{u^{[[n]]+1}}{2([[n]]+1)}\right| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor \( \tfrac{1}{2} \) coming from \( du = 2x\,dx \).
This evaluates the antiderivative only at the upper limit, dropping the lower-limit term.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]} [[c]]\,x\,e^{x^{2}}\,dx \), recognizing \( x \) as (up to a constant) the derivative of the exponent.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the value of \( e^{u} \) at the lower limit \( u=0 \).
This forgets the factor \( \tfrac{1}{2} \) from \( du=2x\,dx \) and doubles instead.
This uses \( \tfrac{1}{4} \) instead of \( \tfrac{1}{2} \) as the leading constant.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This flips the sign of the correct value.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]} \frac{[[c]]\,x}{1 + x^{4}}\,dx \), recognizing \( x \) as (up to a constant) the derivative of \( x^{2} \), the argument of an arctangent form.
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor \( \tfrac{1}{2} \) from \( du = 2x\,dx \).
This doubles the leading constant instead of halving it.
This uses \( \arctan([[a]]) \) instead of \( \arctan([[a]]^{2}) \), forgetting the substitution.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This flips the sign of the correct value.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]} \frac{2\cdot[[c]]\,x}{x^{2} + [[d]]}\,dx \), recognizing the numerator as the derivative of the denominator.
distractor_rationales: This forgets the leading constant \( [[c]] \) and the subtraction of \( \ln([[d]]) \).
This uses \( \ln([[a]]^{2}) \), dropping the \( [[d]] \) shift entirely.
This doubles the correct value.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This flips the sign of the correct value.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]} \frac{[[c]]\,x}{\sqrt{x^{2} + [[d]]}}\,dx \), recognizing the numerator as (up to a constant) the derivative of the quantity under the radical.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the value of \( \sqrt{u} \) at the lower limit \( u=[[d]] \).
This doubles the correct value through a factor-of-2 slip in the substitution.
This subtracts \( [[d]] \) directly instead of \( \sqrt{[[d]]} \).
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This flips the sign of the correct value.
